title: QUIC remaining-work program partition and autopilot reset work item
summary: Record the current remaining QUIC work as three bounded programs, leave the stopped synthesized backlog lane isolated until a later cleanup decision, and retune the autopilot planner so it prefers those hard-gap programs over generic backlog harvesting.
planned_changes:
- Record the 2026-04-18 state snapshot, the outstanding strategic gap families, and the isolated stopped worker lane as non-canonical pending work.
- Partition the remaining work into Program A (post-resumption key lifecycle and 0-RTT truthfulness), Program B (sender/runtime path model), and Program C (stream/public/interop widening gated on A and B).
- Update `prompts/mission.md` so generic backlog synthesis and trace-metadata reconciliation stay subordinate to the remaining hard-gap programs.
- Update `scripts/Invoke-QuicInteropAutopilot.ps1` so the planner exposes a repeatable hard-gap lane and suppresses synthesized backlog lanes while the strategic gap set remains open.
- Refresh the architecture/work-item/verification indexes and generated markdown views so the new planning slice is discoverable from the canonical repo docs.
verification_plan: Validate the new canonical JSON artifacts, render their markdown siblings, regenerate the autopilot plan output, and confirm the recommended lane moves away from synthesized `backlog-*` batches while the current strategic gaps remain open.
out_of_scope: merging, deleting, or rewriting the isolated stopped worker branch and worktree
implementing TLS KeyUpdate, 0-RTT receive or anti-replay, PMTU discovery, token emission, or multi-version stateless reset behavior themselves
widening the public API or interop support claims
counting the isolated synthesized backlog lane as completed progress

supplemental_sections.heading: Program Ownership
supplemental_sections.content:
- Program A keeps the remaining key lifecycle, 0-RTT truthfulness, and discard timing work on one support-honest track.
- Program B keeps the sender/runtime path model gaps together so migration, PMTU, and reset-versioning are treated as one runtime frontier instead of scattered backlog shards.
- Program C stays downstream and must not widen `IsSupported`, transfer expectations, or interop claims until Programs A and B have earned that widening.

completion_notes: Completed the planning and truthfulness slice. It records the remaining split and resets the planner heuristic without claiming new protocol behavior.
